title: Extract Imported SVG To Published Media Delivery
summary: Add a distinct published SVG media type and route sanitized standalone and HTML-inline imports to it through one media plan.
content_html:
Extract Imported SVG To Published Media Delivery

Outcome

Standalone and HTML-inline SVG imports produce sanitized .svg media at the target scope's configured published svg location and compact canonical Markdown image references. No imported SVG markup is retained inline.

The delivery reuses the existing import media plan, location adapter, media-before-source ordering, canonical identity allocation, and coordinated rebuild boundaries.

Delivery Boundary

In scope:

  • normalize standalone and HTML-inline SVG into one sanitized media intent;
  • route both sources through one shared SVG sanitizer that preserves safe self-contained styling and strips external references;
  • register svg as a distinct published media type with logical docs/<scope>/svg/<filename> identities and separate physical folders;
  • validated original basenames for standalone SVG, deterministic <canonical-doc-id>-image-NN.svg identities for HTML-inline SVG, and alt text derived from safe import context;
  • unchanged write-free document preview with all SVG filename, token, destination, collision, and replacement details kept internal;
  • repository publication for repo-backed scopes and contained external-local publication for external-local scopes through their configured svg locations;
  • media-before-source apply, byte verification, source token insertion, rebuild follow-through, and bounded failure reporting;
  • focused ordinary, overwrite, multi-SVG, unsafe-SVG, provider, collision, and partial-failure tests;
  • promotion of the shipped contract into the existing import and media owners.

Out of scope:

  • existing-document migration;
  • manual source-editor media insertion;
  • resolving surrounding HTML CSS, fetching external dependencies, reproducing the browser cascade, or emitting speculative appearance warnings;
  • imported-media version history, rollback copies, or external source-version management;
  • Markdown package SVG-link interpretation unless it already reaches the same normalized inline-SVG boundary;
  • Mermaid source, rendering, freshness checks, or repository tool installation;
  • SVG optimization, visual diffing, or content extraction beyond title/alt assistance.

Code Authority To Inspect

  • docs-viewer/services/docs_html_markdown.py for current SVG parsing/sanitization and HTML conversion, with shared SVG policy moving into a focused sanitizer module;
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_import_preview.py for standalone SVG and HTML preview dispatch;
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_import_media.py for extracted-media planning, deterministic names, tokens, and provider-independent apply;
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_import_document.py and import service orchestration for media-before-source mutation and rebuild follow-through;
  • docs-viewer/services/docs_scope_config.py, scope configuration, local media serving/lifecycle, and inventory for the new published svg type;
  • artifact-location adapters for configured svg resolution and byte verification;
  • focused Docs Import source-format, media, package, collection, and service tests.

These are responsibility areas, not a permanent module inventory.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: planned; standalone and HTML-inline SVG are currently sanitized but serialized directly into canonical Markdown, svg is not yet a registered published media type, and the verified Docs media baseline contains no .svg files requiring migration or compatibility handling.

Next checkpoint: SVG-1 — define one normalized sanitized SVG media plan and keep the ordinary document preview write-free and unchanged.

Complete checkpoints in order. Keep detailed fixtures, generated SVG bodies, and command transcripts outside this delivery.

[ ] SVG-1 — Share Sanitization And Normalize Media Intent

  • [ ] Register the svg published media type and configure repository locations for repo-backed scopes and contained external-local locations for external-local scopes; do not add an initial R2 location.
  • [ ] Extend serving, inventory, and owned-directory lifecycle handling for configured svg locations without adding suffix-based provider branches.
  • [ ] Move SVG parsing, safety policy, external-reference removal, and serialization into one shared sanitizer used by standalone and HTML-inline import.
  • [ ] Return sanitized bytes, title/alt assistance, validity, and non-user-facing removal diagnostics instead of final inline Markdown.
  • [ ] Preserve safe presentation attributes, element styles, internal stylesheets, and surviving same-document fragment references without resolving surrounding HTML CSS or fetching dependencies.
  • [ ] Normalize standalone SVG and each HTML-inline SVG through the same media-plan shape.
  • [ ] Preserve the validated staged basename for standalone SVG and allocate <canonical-doc-id>-image-NN.svg for HTML-inline SVG without writing, including overwrite retargeting and stable multi-SVG document order.
  • [ ] Keep the ordinary document preview write-free and unchanged; do not expose SVG filename, token, provider, physical target, collision, or replacement details through additional preview state or UI.

Checkpoint evidence: the same safe input and target state produces one deterministic internal SVG media plan, unsafe content is absent from its candidate bytes, and ordinary document preview remains write-free.

[ ] SVG-2 — Publish Then Write Source

  • [ ] Publish sanitized SVG bytes through the target scope's configured svg location adapter and byte-verify the result.
  • [ ] Replace standalone or HTML-inline SVG markup with ordinary Markdown image references using the configured logical media prefix.
  • [ ] Preserve the established media-before-source ordering so failed publication cannot commit a broken token.
  • [ ] Treat confirmed document overwrite as authority to replace its stable SVG identities: matching bytes remain unchanged, changed bytes are replaced and verified, no fallback suffix or separate media prompt is introduced, and an unrelated new-document media collision blocks.

Checkpoint evidence: repository and external-local fixtures produce the same logical svg token while only the configured physical location changes; no R2 or suffix-specific import branch exists.

[ ] SVG-3 — Integrate Import And Failure Semantics

  • [ ] Carry SVG media plans internally through ordinary import preview/apply and the shared per-document boundary used by collection imports where applicable without adding SVG-specific response fields.
  • [ ] Report malformed input and sanitization, publication, collision, source-write, and rebuild failures separately without treating possible CSS-dependent appearance changes as warnings.
  • [ ] Ensure partial media publication or source-write failure leaves an explicit result and never claims an unverified token was committed.
  • [ ] Keep imported document identity, timestamps, hierarchy, viewability defaults, and rebuild behavior unchanged apart from SVG representation.
  • [ ] Leave surplus numbered SVG files from a smaller re-import unreferenced without automatic deletion, reconciliation, or user warning.

Checkpoint evidence: an SVG publication failure stops the matching source write, while a later rebuild failure does not erase successfully committed source and media.

[ ] SVG-4 — Verify, Document, And Close

  • [ ] Prove standalone, HTML-inline, multiple, titled, untitled, unsafe, externally referenced, internally styled, fragment-referenced, and malformed SVG cases at the lowest stable layers.
  • [ ] Prove standalone basename preservation, deterministic HTML-inline numbering, unchanged-byte reuse, confirmed-overwrite replacement, unrelated collision refusal, absence of fallback suffixes, provider-neutral tokens, byte verification, and compact resulting Markdown.
  • [ ] Prove existing inline-raster extraction and ordinary image/file import behavior remain unchanged.
  • [ ] Manually compare representative SVG before and after import; visual fidelity remains a review decision rather than an automated import guarantee.
  • [ ] After implementation, promote scope-plus-media-type policy and svg topology into Source Organisation, author best practice into Docs Images And Assets, the SVG media/sanitization contract into Media And Asset Handling, and deterministic conversion into Docs Import Architecture; rebuild Studio docs/search, run git diff --check, and run the focused private-path/secret scan.

Checkpoint evidence: imported source contains media tokens rather than SVG markup, every referenced SVG exists at its registered published location, and existing non-SVG import behavior remains green.

Done When

  • standalone and HTML-inline SVG imports publish sanitized .svg media and write compact logical image tokens;
  • svg is a distinct configured published media type with separate logical and physical roots from raster img;
  • both paths use one shared sanitizer, retain safe self-contained styling, strip external references, and do not resolve surrounding HTML CSS;
  • standalone publication preserves the validated staged basename while HTML-inline extraction uses deterministic document-owned numbered SVG filenames;
  • unsafe original SVG bytes are never published or committed to canonical Markdown;
  • target scope-plus-media-type configuration alone chooses placement, initially repository for repo-backed scopes and contained external-local storage for external-local scopes;
  • media publication and byte verification precede source mutation;
  • naming, collisions, overwrite retargeting, partial failures, and rebuild results are explicit and tested;
  • confirmed document re-import replaces stable SVG media without a separate prompt, fallback filename, history, or rollback copy;
  • ordinary document preview remains the only preview, with no SVG-specific media details or handling UI;
  • smaller re-imports may leave quiet unreferenced SVG files for later provider-independent inventory rather than automatic cleanup;
  • possible CSS-dependent appearance changes do not create speculative warnings, and representative results receive before/after visual review;
  • import offers one deterministic published-media representation while manually authored inline SVG remains an intentional exception;
  • current raster img extraction and non-SVG imports remain unchanged;
  • durable import and media documentation describes the shipped behavior.
